assertions.title: Authenticated requests receive a Bedrock or Z.AI response through the gateway

steps.action: Register test user via WebAuthn using Playwright virtual authenticator
steps.expected: User registered and authenticated session established

steps.action: Navigate to deployed origin with auth cookies
steps.expected: Authenticated shell displayed with prompt interface
steps.observed: Reached authenticated shell with 'Ask the runtime...' prompt textbox and launcher button

steps.expected: Task submitted to /api/agent/task and processed through gateway

steps.action: Monitor network traffic for direct provider calls
steps.expected: All traffic stays within draft.choir-ip.com origin, no direct browser→provider calls
steps.observed: All requests to draft.choir-ip.com: /api/agent/task, /api/events, /api/agent/status. No requests to bedrock.amazonaws.com, api.z.ai, or other provider origins.

steps.action: Verify task completion through same-origin response path
steps.expected: Response returned through gateway → runtime → proxy → UI
steps.observed: Task status returned 'completed' with result through /api/agent/status endpoint on same origin

evidence.consoleErrors: [error] Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 502 () - occurred during initial prompt submission attempt
network: No requests to external provider endpoints (bedrock.amazonaws.com, api.z.ai, etc.)

assertions.issues: Gateway provider integration is not yet complete. The runtime returns stub responses 'Task completed successfully (stub provider)' rather than routing through Bedrock/Z.AI. The path login → proxy → runtime → UI is working, but the gateway → provider segment returns stub responses. This is expected as the gateway milestone is still in progress.

frictions.description: WebAuthn virtual authenticator requires Playwright CDP, not available directly in agent-browser
frictions.resolution: Used Playwright for WebAuthn registration, then transferred cookies to agent-browser for UI testing

frictions.description: Auth cookies expired quickly during browser session, causing repeated sign-outs
frictions.resolution: Used curl with explicit cookie headers for API verification after browser session expired

frictions.description: Runtime returns stub provider responses instead of real Bedrock/Z.AI responses
frictions.resolution: Pending gateway milestone completion - the routing path is established but provider integration is stubbed

blockers.description: Gateway provider integration is incomplete - runtime returns stub responses 'Task completed successfully (stub provider)' rather than routing to Bedrock or Z.AI. The proxy health shows 'vmctl_routing: enabled' but sandbox is still 'sandbox-m1' (placeholder) rather than a VM with real gateway access.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified the path login → proxy → runtime → UI works correctly. The missing piece is gateway → provider integration which is scheduled for the gateway milestone.

summary: Tested VAL-GATEWAY-001: The authenticated prompt flow through login → proxy → runtime → UI is working correctly with all network traffic staying within draft.choir-ip.com origin (no direct provider calls from browser). However, the gateway → Bedrock/Z.AI segment is not yet integrated - the runtime returns stub responses. The assertion is BLOCKED pending completion of the gateway provider integration milestone.
